name: Corn-Meal Ice-Box Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 Cake Compressed Yeast             1/2 cup Lukewarm Water
OR 1  Cake Dry Yeast                4 cups Boiling Water
1 Cup White Corn-Meal                     1 Cup Melted Shortening
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix yeast, 1/2 cup water, and 3 tablespoons flour in a bowl. Cover and let stand until light.
2. Slowly sift corn-meal into boiling water. Add salt and stir constantly until thick. Cook over hot water for 30 minutes.
3. Stir in melted shortening, sugar, and potato water (boil 1 cup diced potatoes in 2 cups of water). Blend well.
4. Cool mixture. Add the yeast sponge to the dough. Add enough flour to make a dough that is stiff enough to knead. Turn onto lightly floured surface. Knead until smooth and elastic. Cover with damp cloth. Let rise until doubled in bulk.
5. Remove portion of dough from refrigerator 2 hours or more before rolling out. Allow dough to warm to room temperature. Roll into rolls. Place in well-oiled pans. Cover and let rise until tripled in bulk.
6. Bake in hot oven (450°F) about 15 minutes. Unused portion should be kneaded down, covered closely with waxed paper, and returned to refrigerator until needed. Makes approximately 75 rolls.
